steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is “dictated by other men”—we need evidence that the group has power over Newland's behavior, not that he has superiority over them.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D directly illustrates this power dynamic: “the habit of masculine solidarity made him accept their doctrine” shows the men's collective influence compelling his conformity.

The other three either address unrelated concerns (the Opera House in A, his internal awareness in B) or reverse the relationship (C portrays his intellectual superiority rather than the men's power over him).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
